title: WITHDRAWN — div_ceil off-by-one: code has .expect() panic (not silent corruption), 1 SF quantum ≈ $0 impact. Not submission-worthy.
description: div_ceil formula ((num_sf << 60) + denum_sf - 1) / denum_sf is incorrect for fixed-point ceiling division. On exact integer ratios (e.g., 1.0 / 1.0), returns 2^60 + 1 instead of 2^60. Same pattern in BigFraction::div_ceil and full_mul_int_ratio_ceil. Callers: fraction_liquidity_to_collateral_ceil, fraction_collateral_to_liquidity_ceil, lending_operations.rs:513.
impact: 1 U68F60 quantum per ceiling-divided liquidation (~8.67e-19 relative). Financial magnitude is below any exploitable threshold — no dollar value lost or gained. Systematic but negligible. Downgraded from CRITICAL.
recommendation: Replace with ((num_sf << FRAC_NBITS) - 1) / denum_sf + 1. Correctness matters for code discipline in a $100M+ protocol even at quantum scale.

title: WITHDRAWN — u128 multiplication: .expect() guard + overflow mathematically impossible at Kamino params (2^60 * u32::MAX = 2^92 << u128::MAX)
description: nom = coef * u128::from(self.slope_nom) with no checked_mul. Direct overflow at 100% util requires slope_nom ~2^38 (impractical); normal kamino params are safe. REAL danger: if coef is PRE-COMPUTED from upstream arithmetic that overflows silently (e.g., interpolated coef from (util-start)*segment_scale), the wrapped value arrives already wrong. to_bps().unwrap() also discards fractional BPS via .round(), underestimating by up to 0.5 BPS.
impact: Wrapped coef produces garbage borrow rate. Silent propagation: no panic, no revert. All borrows on affected reserve accrue wrong rate. Must halt reserve to repair. Secondary: to_bps() loses up to 0.5 BPS per calculation systematically.
recommendation: Use checked_mul: nom = coef.checked_mul(slope_nom.into())? Return error on overflow so tx reverts gracefully. Audit all upstream coef computations. Fix to_bps().unwrap() to handle fractional BPS explicitly.

title: redeem_fees — no signer check, any transaction can trigger fee withdrawal via PDA-signed CPI
description: RedeemFees accounts struct has zero Signer accounts. The lending_market_authority PDA signs the token transfer via invoke_signed. Any transaction — any signer, any program — can call redeem_fees and trigger a token transfer. The fee_receiver (defaulted to lending_market_authority) receives fees, so funds cannot be redirected to arbitrary addresses, but timing and sequencing can be manipulated.
impact: MEV front-running of fee collection. Forced fee collection timing. Any account can invoke redeem_fees at any point in any transaction. No fund loss but unexpected state changes possible.
recommendation: Add lending_market_owner signer to RedeemFees accounts struct. Alternatively emit event and require off-chain monitoring of redeem_fees invocations.

title: Global admin sets pending_admin to themselves — two-step pattern is cosmetic
description: update_global_config(UpdatePendingAdmin) allows global_admin to set pending_admin to any value including themselves. update_global_config_admin (apply) only requires pending_admin signature. The same entity controlling both keys can complete the full takeover in two transactions with no additional authorization, no delay, no notification. Global admin key compromise = full protocol takeover.
impact: Compromised global_admin key: attacker sets pending_admin=self in tx1, applies in tx2. Full protocol admin takeover with no additional auth. Owner of global_config can seize all protocol funds and config.
recommendation: Require current global_admin to sign update_global_config_admin.apply_pending_admin(). Alternatively introduce mandatory timelock between setting pending_admin and applying it.

title: permissioning_authority change has no timelock — insider/timelock-free takeover path
description: Lending market owner can call UpdateLendingMarket(UpdatePermissioningAuthority) to redirect permissioning_authority to attacker-controlled key with no timelock, no multisig, no notification. The new authority can immediately exercise DEPOSIT, BORROW, LIQUIDATE on all permissioned reserves. The immutable flag does not block this — attack executes before setting immutable, or if immutable was never set.
impact: Malicious lending_market_owner (or compromised key) silently redirects permissioning_authority and operates on restricted reserves without triggering any on-chain event or notification. Insider threat / key-compromise scenario. External attackers cannot exploit unless they own the owner key.
recommendation: Add mandatory timelock (24-48h) between UpdatePermissioningAuthority and activation. Emit PermissioningAuthorityChanging event. Consider multisig requirement for permissioning_authority changes. Add UpdatePermissioningAuthority to immutable market exclusion list.

title: Divide-by-zero panic on zero oracle price in max_liquidatable_borrowed_amount
description: max_liquidation_ratio = max_liquidatable_mv / obligation_debt_for_liquidity_mv. obligation_debt_for_liquidity_mv = liquidity.market_value() — oracle price * borrowed_amount. If oracle price is exactly zero (stale, delisted, acute crash), denominator is zero and plain / panics. Near-zero price produces enormous ratio; repay_amount * ratio causes integer overflow or grossly incorrect amounts.
impact: Zero oracle price: DoS on all liquidation for that reserve. Near-zero oracle price: grossly incorrect liquidation amounts. No attacker control needed — normal market condition path.
recommendation: Guard with if obligation_debt_for_liquidity_mv.is_zero() { return Fraction::ZERO; } or checked_div with error propagation. Add staleness check on oracle prices before entering liquidation logic.

title: BigFraction::mul silent U256 wrapping on overflow — no guard
description: self.0 * rhs.0 (U256 * U256 → U256) uses uint crate multiplication which wraps silently on overflow. No checked_mul, no expect, no panic. Results propagate silently as garbage values into cumulative borrow rates and market values.
impact: Obligation borrow rate or market value approaching U256 maximum (extreme scenarios) silently produces wrong values. No panic, no revert — corrupted state. Impact depends on financial magnitude of affected positions.
recommendation: Use checked_mul from uint crate. Audit all BigFraction callers for financial significance. Implement overflow detection returning error or panic rather than wrapping.

title: Three LTV functions panic on zero collateral — DoS vector
description: loan_to_value(), no_bf_loan_to_value(), unhealthy_loan_to_value() use plain / operator on deposited_value_sf. Zero collateral (deposited_value_sf == 0) triggers panic. Callers include check_liquidate_obligation, autodeleveraging checks, ObligationOrder evaluation. No zero-denominator guard in any caller.
impact: Obligation reaching zero deposited collateral through normal liquidation triggers panic on next refresh. All operations (borrow, deposit, liquidation) on that obligation blocked. No attacker control needed — normal path.
recommendation: Add if self.deposited_value_sf == 0 { return Fraction::MAX; } guard to each function. Or use checked_div handling None as Fraction::MAX (maximum LTV = fully liquidated). Audit all callers for preconditions.

title: Liquidation rounding asymmetry — repay rounds UP, collateral rounds DOWN
description: In Ordering::Less branch: repay_amount = debt_liquidation_amount.to_ceil() (rounds UP) but withdraw_amount = deposited_amount * (total_liquidation_value / collateral_value).to_floor() (rounds DOWN). Liquidator systematically pays ceiling-rounded debt and receives floor-rounded collateral. MATH-003-1 (broken div_ceil) amplifies the repay ceiling. repay_amount can exceed borrowed_amount when total_liquidation_value_including_bonus slightly exceeds collateral_value.
impact: Liquidators systematically lose value on partial liquidations near the collateral threshold. Over many liquidations, creates a subsidy at liquidators' expense, potentially discouraging participation. Protocol collects less than configured fees.
recommendation: Align rounding: use to_floor() for repay_amount, or compute repay_amount = min(ceil(debt_liquidation_amount), borrowed_amount). Audit all liquidation rounding paths.

title: Emergency council can enable borrow-disable + liquidation-disable without lending market owner
description: Emergency council can call UpdateLendingMarket with UpdateBorrowingDisabled (value=true) or UpdatePriceTriggeredLiquidationDisabled (value=true) without lending_market_owner approval. emergency_council is set by lending_market_owner via UpdateEmergencyCouncil (owner-only). Emergency council key compromise combined with malicious oracle creates maximum-damage scenario.
impact: Compromised emergency_council key + malicious oracle: borrowing halted, liquidation disabled, user funds at risk. Realistic threat model for multisig-equivalent deployments.
recommendation: Require lending_market_owner to co-sign emergency council actions. Add rate-limit on emergency flag toggles. Consider circuit-breaker on combined enable/disable patterns.

title: withdraw_protocol_fee has no signer — any tx triggers fee withdrawal
description: WithdrawProtocolFees requires no signer. fee_collector is set by global_admin. Any transaction triggers fee withdrawal to fee_collector ATA. PDA (lending_market_authority) signs the token transfer. Funds go to fee_collector ATA (not redirectable to arbitrary address) but collection can be forced at any time.
impact: MEV front-running fee collection timing. Forced fee sweeps. Any tx can trigger. Cannot redirect funds. Low direct fund risk but unexpected state changes possible.
recommendation: Require global_admin or fee_collector to sign withdraw_protocol_fee instruction. Emit events on fee collection for off-chain monitoring.

title: socialize_loss requires only lending_market_owner — no global_admin co-sign
description: SocializeLoss and SocializeLossV2 distribute bad debt across all obligation owners but only require lending_market_owner signature. Compromised lending_market_owner can call socialize_loss to transfer collateral from healthy positions to cover bad debt inappropriately, or exaggerate losses onto healthy depositors.
impact: Malicious or compromised lending_market_owner: socialize_loss used to steal collateral from healthy positions. Realistic threat model for owner-key deployments.
recommendation: Require both lending_market_owner and global_admin to co-sign socialize_loss. Add threshold for socialize operations (e.g., minimum bad debt ratio). Emit events on socialize calls.

title: Liquidation bonus formula computes 4.762% not 5% — liquidators systematically underpaid
description: bonus = amount_liquidated - (amount_liquidated / bonus_multiplier) where bonus_multiplier=1.05 computes amount * (1 - 1/1.05) = amount * 0.047619, not amount * 0.05. Liquidators receive 4.762% bonus instead of 5.0%. Protocol fee is then 20% of bonus = 0.952%. max(protocol_fee, 1) also forces 1-lamport minimum even when protocol_liquidation_fee_pct=0.
impact: Liquidators systematically earn 4.76% less per liquidation than intended. Reduces liquidation incentive at margin. In competitive liquidation markets, may discourage liquidations on small/narrow positions, causing healthy positions to drift further into insolvency. $2,381 leak per $1M liquidation at 5% rate. No protocol fund loss but incentive misalignment is real.
recommendation: Replace with amount_liquidated * liquidation_bonus_rate. Conditionally apply max(protocol_fee, 1) only when protocol_liquidation_fee_pct > 0.

title: Sharp close-factor cliff at insolvency boundary — oracle nudge exploit possible
description: Obligation at exactly insolvency_risk_unhealthy_ltv threshold gets 20% close factor; one quantum above gets 100%. Sharp cliff creates incentive to nudge obligation just past boundary via oracle manipulation to maximize liquidation impact. Liquidator can be anyone; oracle nudge cost depends on asset.
impact: Oracle-nudgable attacker (cost depends on oracle) can push near-threshold obligation from 20% to 100% close. Exploitability depends on oracle attack cost. Contained to liquidatable obligations near boundary.
recommendation: Use >= for boundary check, or smooth transition with gradient. Add circuit-breaker on oracle price change rate per obligation.

title: 1-SF gap between max_withdraw and unhealthy LTV boundary
description: max_withdraw_value subtracts 1 SF unit from unhealthy_borrow_value_sf (saturating_sub(1)). Liquidation boundary check uses >= on unhealthy_loan_to_value. Obligation exactly at unhealthy threshold: liquidatable=true but max_withdraw=0. Broken div_ceil (MATH-003-1) amplifies this gap.
impact: Architecturally inconsistent — two paths produce conflicting results at boundary. Negligible financial impact (1 SF ≈ 8.67e-19 USD at typical magnitudes). Compounds with MATH-003-1.
recommendation: Align both paths. Remove saturating_sub(1) in max_withdraw, or change liquidation boundary from >= to >.

title: Proposer authority configures reserves before proposer_authority_locked — initialization phase risk
description: Proposer_authority (distinct from lending_market_owner) can call update_reserve_config during initialization phase before proposer_authority_locked=true. init_lending_market requires only lending_market_owner signer but does not check for existing lending market at PDA. Proposer can set unfavorable initial configurations.
impact: Contained to initialization phase. Exploitable only if initialization sequence has weak access controls or proposer_authority is distributed insecurely. Normal deployments: low risk.
recommendation: Ensure proposer_authority_locked is set immediately after initialization phase completes. Audit initialization sequences for access control completeness.

title: BigFraction::from_num overflow panic guard unreliable for large inputs
description: panicking_shl(value, 60) detects overflow at >=2^60 but uint << on U256 may wrap before guard check fires. Overflow may silently produce wrong values instead of panicking.
impact: Contained — financial amounts in lending context unlikely to reach 10^18 units of smallest denomination for most assets. Theoretical for standard token mints. Real impact only for extreme edge cases.
recommendation: Use checked_shl from uint crate or implement explicit overflow detection returning error rather than panicking. Audit all BigFraction construction paths.
